jokes.text: Look what the homosexuals have done to me.
jokes.setup: Lucille is upset at husband's retirement party
jokes.punchline: She blames gay protesters for ruining her hair
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment establishing Lucille's self-centered worldview and casual bigotry. Absurd logic delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Everything they do is so dramatic and flamboyant. It just makes me want to set myself on fire.
jokes.setup: Lucille complaining about dramatic gay protesters
jokes.punchline: Her response is the most dramatic thing imaginable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - she criticizes dramatics with the most dramatic statement possible. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I have the exact same blouse.
jokes.setup: Lindsay sees gay protester
jokes.punchline: She's shocked they have matching clothes
jokes.explanation: Establishes Lindsay's vanity and self-absorption - even in this situation, it's about her.

jokes.text: I like it better on him.
jokes.setup: Lindsay notices matching blouse
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that gay protester looks better
jokes.explanation: Understated follow-up that shows Lindsay's honesty about her own appearance.

jokes.text: Illusion, Michael. A trick is something a whore does for money. Or candy.
jokes.setup: Michael calls it a magic trick
jokes.punchline: Gob's pretentious correction gets absurd with 'or candy' addition
jokes.explanation: Iconic Gob moment. Perfect character beat - his pretension undercut by realizing kids might be listening. The 'or candy' addition is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Hasn't everything already sort of been discovered, though... by, like, Magellan and Cortés? Those guys did a pretty good job...
jokes.setup: Narrator explains Buster studies cartography
jokes.punchline: Buster points out the obvious flaw in studying 'uncharted territories'
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's naive logic applied to his ridiculous education. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: He also suffers from crippling panic attacks.
jokes.setup: Buster's awkward social interaction
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact revelation of serious condition
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator timing - drops serious information casually for comedic effect.

jokes.text: Because he's decided to never speak to these people again.
jokes.setup: Why is Michael so happy?
jokes.punchline: He's cutting off his family (whom we just met)
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff that subverts family sitcom expectations while establishing central tension.

jokes.text: Breakfast. Family. Right. I thought you meant of the things you eat.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what comes before anything
jokes.punchline: George Michael's literal interpretation reveals his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage boy logic - food is genuinely more important to him than abstract concepts.

jokes.text: I just remembered something. Dad's retiring, not turning six.
jokes.setup: Gob wants to perform magic at retirement party
jokes.punchline: Michael points out age-inappropriate entertainment
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical putdown that highlights Gob's obliviousness to appropriate contexts.

jokes.text: I love all my children equally. I don't care for Gob.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims to love all children equally
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself in same breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction delivered without awareness. Brilliant character moment showing Lucille's complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: I'm hoping the universe provides a path for me.
jokes.setup: Asked about job search
jokes.punchline: Tobias gives new-age non-answer instead of actual effort
jokes.explanation: Establishes Tobias's pretentious, passive approach to life problems.

jokes.text: Maybe you'll be inspired by the boat party tonight, start a career as a pirate.
jokes.setup: Tobias needs career inspiration
jokes.punchline: Michael sarcastically suggests piracy based on boat setting
jokes.explanation: Sets up Tobias's later pirate costume misunderstanding. Gains value on rewatch.

jokes.text: I haven't packed for that.
jokes.setup: Michael jokingly suggests pirate career
jokes.punchline: Tobias takes it literally and practically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias response - literal-minded and earnest about absurd suggestion. Sets up his costume plot.

jokes.text: Sounds like you saved enough skin to make 10 new boys.
jokes.setup: Lindsay raised $40,000 for anti-circumcision cause
jokes.punchline: Michael makes disturbing calculation about foreskin quantity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate calculation that's both clever and disturbing. Perfect Michael response.

jokes.text: Mom says it's too windy.
jokes.setup: Michael tells Buster to juice on the balcony
jokes.punchline: Adult man cites mother's permission for basic activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Buster's infantilization and dependence on Lucille.

jokes.text: It tasted like a foot. Which I didn't really mind, but I'm pretty sure I said no nuts.
jokes.setup: Customer found foot in frozen banana
jokes.punchline: Accepts foot taste but draws line at nuts
jokes.explanation: Absurd priority system - foot taste acceptable, nuts are not. Brilliant escalation.

jokes.text: And then she'll see you and me totally making out. But not really, right?
jokes.setup: Maeby suggests fake cousin romance to shock parents
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent clarification reveals his horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Maeby's cynical manipulation vs George Michael's innocent panic.

jokes.text: Tobias boarded a van full of homosexuals.
jokes.setup: Tobias mistakes gay protesters for pirates
jokes.punchline: He accidentally joins gay rights protest
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy setup that pays off throughout episode. Tobias's confusion creates multiple layers.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I have the exact same blouse. I like it better on him.
jokes.setup: Earlier identical exchange with different gay man
jokes.punchline: Exact repetition with new context
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals this happens to Lindsay regularly. Shows her predictable vanity.

jokes.text: My lovely wife, Lucille.
jokes.setup: George Sr. describes 'smartest, favorite, sexiest' new CEO
jokes.punchline: It's his wife, not Michael
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that devastates Michael's expectations. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna lie to you, Michael. This is great news for HOOP.
jokes.setup: Michael is devastated by not getting promotion
jokes.punchline: Lindsay only cares how it benefits her cause
jokes.explanation: Perfect selfish response showing Lindsay's complete lack of empathy for her brother.

jokes.text: I knew it was against the law.
jokes.setup: Police boats approaching party
jokes.punchline: George Michael thinks it's about cousin kissing scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing George Michael's guilt about innocent scheme while real crimes unfold.

jokes.text: Obviously this blue part here is the land.
jokes.setup: Buster asked to navigate using his cartography knowledge
jokes.punchline: He thinks blue areas on map represent land
jokes.explanation: Pays off his expensive education with fundamental misunderstanding of basic map conventions.

jokes.text: Illusions, Dad! You don't have time for my illusions!
jokes.setup: George Sr. dismisses Gob's 'magic tricks' during crisis
jokes.punchline: Gob maintains his pretentious terminology even while being rejected
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Gob's priorities - even during family crisis, terminology matters most.

jokes.text: I thought that the homosexuals were pirates... but it turns out that most of them were actors in the local theater.
jokes.setup: Tobias explains his arrest
jokes.punchline: Multiple layers of mistaken identity and stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Layered confusion showing Tobias's complete misunderstanding of every situation he encountered.

jokes.text: You're gay? No. No. I'm not... I'm not gay. No. Lindsay, how many times must we have this... No. I want to be an actor.
jokes.setup: Tobias inspired by theater experience
jokes.punchline: His defensive denial suggests ongoing marital suspicions
jokes.explanation: Establishes ongoing marital tension and Tobias's sexuality questions that become major plot points.

jokes.text: Buster? The guy who thought that the blue on the map was land?
jokes.setup: Lucille puts Buster in charge of company
jokes.punchline: Michael references his recent navigation failure
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that reinforces why Buster shouldn't run anything important.

jokes.text: Eighteenth-century agrarian business. But I guess it's all the same principles.
jokes.setup: Buster claims to have business education
jokes.punchline: His knowledge is completely irrelevant to modern business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster logic - applying 18th century farming principles to modern real estate development.

jokes.text: And this is 'I'm a Bad, Bad Man' from Annie Get Your Gun.
jokes.setup: Tobias lists impressive medical credentials
jokes.punchline: Follows with completely inappropriate audition song choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of professional credentials with terrible artistic judgment. Classic Tobias.

jokes.text: The man can barely work our shredder.
jokes.setup: SEC thinks George Sr. is criminal mastermind
jokes.punchline: Lucille says he's incompetent with basic office equipment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucille's contempt for her husband while inadvertently undermining his defense.

jokes.text: We need ice. Michael. We need Michael.
jokes.setup: Business meeting discussing complex figures
jokes.punchline: Buster's childish panic and priorities
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's complete inability to handle business responsibility - reduces to basic needs.

jokes.text: F off, traitor.
jokes.setup: Gob being blackballed by magician organization
jokes.punchline: Even the replacement magician insults him
jokes.explanation: Shows how seriously the magic community takes their rules and how universally disliked Gob is.

jokes.text: Well, I'd rather be dead in California than alive in Arizona.
jokes.setup: Michael threatening to move to Arizona
jokes.punchline: Lucille's dramatic preference for death
jokes.explanation: Classic California snobbery joke elevated by Lucille's characteristic melodrama.

jokes.text: They cannot arrest a husband and wife... for the same crime.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explains why he made Lucille CEO
jokes.punchline: Completely false legal understanding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake legal fact delivered with complete confidence. Became an iconic quote and running gag.

jokes.text: I got the worst... attorneys.
jokes.setup: Michael points out George's legal theory is wrong
jokes.punchline: George's delayed realization about his lawyers' competence
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan realization that became a quotable moment. The pause makes it work.

jokes.text: Well, since Mom died, it's been kind of lonely...
jokes.setup: George Michael wants family to stay
jokes.punchline: Reveals his mother's death casually in conversation
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking backstory delivered matter-of-factly, showing why he craves family connection.

jokes.text: J.D.L.? Jewish Defense League. Oh, the circumcision thing?
jokes.setup: Lindsay mentions being pursued by J.D.L.
jokes.punchline: Her anti-circumcision activism has attracted organized opposition
jokes.explanation: Escalates Lindsay's ridiculous cause to having real-world consequences.

jokes.text: Seriously, you're gonna pull a muscle.
jokes.setup: Lindsay attempting to cry
jokes.punchline: Michael treats her emotional effort like physical exercise
jokes.explanation: Clever observation that Lindsay's emotional displays are so forced they're physically strenuous.

jokes.text: He should've been stocking up on those 'get out of jail free' cards.
jokes.setup: Playing Monopoly, George Sr. always won as banker
jokes.punchline: George Michael connects game to real situation
jokes.explanation: Sweet innocent joke connecting board game to real-world consequences. Shows George Michael's gentle humor.
